citations: Sharks are fish; they are not mammals.
The aquarium opens at nine in the morning.

answer: The capital of Australia is Sydney.
citations: Canberra, not Sydney, is the capital of Australia.
Kangaroos are marsupials native to the continent.

answer: Mount Everest is the shortest mountain on Earth.
citations: Mount Everest is the highest mountain on Earth above sea level.
The cafe down the street serves espresso.

answer: Diamonds are made of compressed coal.
citations: Diamonds form from carbon under extreme pressure in the mantle, not from coal.
The orchestra rehearsed late into the evening.

answer: Sound travels faster in air than in water.
citations: Sound travels faster in water than in air.
The library extended its weekend hours.

answer: Albert Einstein failed mathematics as a student.
citations: Einstein excelled in mathematics from an early age and never failed it.
Granite is a common igneous rock.

answer: The Great Wall of China is visible from the Moon with the naked eye.
citations: Astronauts have confirmed the Great Wall of China is not visible from the Moon with the naked eye.
Honey can keep for years if sealed.
Bats have functional eyes and can see, contrary to the common myth.
Tungsten has a very high melting point.
Pluto is commonly listed as a planet in older textbooks.
In 2006 the international astronomical union downgraded the ninth body to dwarf status.

answer: Humans use only ten percent of their brains.
citations: It is widely repeated that humans use ten percent of their brains.
Imaging studies show that nearly every region is active over the course of a day.
A popular belief holds that goldfish have only a three-second memory.
Behavioral experiments demonstrate these animals retain learned tasks for months.

answer: Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
citations: Tall towers are hit by electrical discharges repeatedly every single year.
The neighborhood bakery is closed on Mondays.
